headline: Wall Street Banks Try Out Anthropic’s Mythos as US Urges
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Wall Street banks are starting to test Anthropic PBC’s Mythos model internally as Trump administration officials encourage them to use it to detect vulnerabilities.While JPMorgan Chase & Co. was the only bank named as part of an initiative to test the Mythos model, other major financial institutions have also gained access or expect to in the coming days, according to people familiar with the matter. Goldman Sachs Group Inc., Citigroup Inc., Bank of America Corp. and Morgan Stanle

headline: Stats Perform wraps B-minus rated leveraged loan at lofty 12.35% yield
summary: Stats Perform has completed its $475 million, four-year covenant-lite term loan B at S+700, with a 0% floor and an OID of 96.5 through a Morgan Stanley-led arranger group, according to sources. The yield-to-maturity on the loan is 12.35%. Proceeds will be used to refinance the company’s existing credit facilities alongside a concurrent $275 million equity contribution from the sponsor. The company will repay a fully drawn $62 million revolver, $471 million of a covenant-lite first-lien term loan

headline: How The Experian (LSE:EXPN) Story Is Shifting As Targets Tighten And New Products Launch
summary: Experian’s fair value estimate has been nudged from £42.42 to £42.31, a small shift that still matters if you are tracking how analysts are fine tuning their expectations. Bullish commentary links this move to tweaks in inputs rather than a rethink of the business model, while more cautious voices highlight the reduced headroom implied by the lower price target. As you read on, you will see how these subtle adjustments fit into the broader narrative and how to keep on top of future...

headline: APO, KKR, OWL Confront $20B Private-Credit Pullout Demands
summary: The scale of the exit rush that rocked private credit funds during the first quarter is now known. According to calculations by the Financial Times, investors attempted to yank more than $20 billion from such funds during the period, as worries mounted over lending to the software industry, a sector hit hardest by recent jitters that AI could render them obsolete. Several industry titans were impacted by this dash for the exits, including Blackstone (BX), KKR (KKR), Apollo Global Management (APO
